title: Insurance Policy Q&A Agent
problem: Policyholders and agents spend hours on hold or navigating PDFs to get answers about coverage, exclusions, and claim eligibility.
solution: RAG agent over a versioned policy document knowledge base. Retrieves relevant policy sections, validates freshness, generates grounded answers with section citations.
workflowSteps: User submits natural language question about their policy
Query is embedded and run against the policy vector index
Pre-filter applies by policy type and policyholder entitlement
Top-k chunks retrieved, freshness checked against last-updated date
LLM generates answer grounded in retrieved sections with citations
Response includes policy section reference and last-updated date
keyInsights: Document freshness is critical in insurance — a stale exclusion clause answer can create liability. Freshness gate + 'policy last updated on X' disclaimer is mandatory, not optional.

relatedExams.why: Applications and Integration — RAG pipeline design
Prompt and Context Engineering — context window management

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([User Question]) --> B[Embed Query]
  B --> C[RBAC Pre-Filter by Policy Type]
  C --> D[Vector Search Top-K]
  D --> E{Freshness Gate}
  E -->|Current| F[LLM Answer with Citations]
  E -->|Stale| G[Staleness Disclaimer + LLM Answer]
  F & G --> H([Grounded Answer])
architectureNotes: Policy documents are chunked at the section level (not page) to preserve logical boundaries, then embedded and stored in a vector index. At query time, a query rewriter normalises insurance jargon before retrieval. Top-k chunks are reranked by section relevance and recency. The LLM answers with section citations; a confidence scorer flags low-confidence answers for human escalation.
